_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Emergency tendons repair ± Peripheral Nerve repair / reconstructive surgery
procedure_name: Emergency tendons repair ± Peripheral Nerve repair / reconstructive surgery
clinical_indications: Clean cuts with underlying tendon injury (early repair from Emergency OT advised for optimal results)
Contaminated wounds with underlying tendon injury, after adequate wound debridement (repair considered after 48 hours re-assessment)
Gross tissue loss and exposed tendons, after adequate wound debridement and soft tissue cover (delayed repair of tendon injuries considered)
Root avulsion or laceration injury to the plexus suspected, or open injury, retained foreign body, or vascular injury requiring vessel repair (urgent surgical exploration indicated)
Sharp transections of the brachial plexus (primary repair allowed to optimize nerve regeneration)
Failure to regain neurologic function satisfactorily after 3-6 months post-injury (delayed exploration may be indicated)

preauth.label: Clinical Notes detailing the injury and need for surgery
Medico legal case report/FIR copy of accident, if due to accident

ppd_preauth.q: Were the clinical notes suggestive of tendon injury with or without nerve repair?
Clinical notes details of accident, signs & symptoms, indication for surgery, and planned line of treatment?
Was the Medico legal report/ FIR copy of the accident submitted, if the injury was due to accident?
Was the nerve conductional velocity report/ EMG report/ MRI report submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Were the indoor case papers submitted?
Are the detailed Procedure/ Operation notes submitted?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes not suggestive of tendon injury with or without nerve repair.
Clinical notes missing details of accident, signs & symptoms, indication for surgery, or planned line of treatment.
Medico legal report/FIR copy of accident missing when injury due to accident.
Optional investigative reports (e.g., nerve conduction velocity, EMG, MRI) not submitted when clinically indicated for pre-authorization review.
Detailed Discharge Summary not submitted or does not mention date of follow-up.

clinical_key_pointers: Patient usually presents with c/o inability to bend or extend a finger
To be ruled out in cuts involving fingers, palm and dorsum of hand, wrist and forearm
Mechanism of injury to be noted
Look whether it's a clean or contaminated wound
FDS, FDP, FPL, FCR, FCU to be tested and zones of flexor tendon injury to be noted
APL, EPB, ECRL, ERCB, EPL, EI, EDC, EDM, EhCU to be tested and zones of extensor tendon injury to be noted
Vascularity of the affected finger/hand to be documented by capillary refill or pinprick
X-ray of the involved part to rule out fractures and foreign body
Associated vascular or nerve injury to be documented
Obtaining a thorough history in a patient with possible NPI is necessary to identify the injury mechanism, associated fractures, and other concomitant trauma to the neck, shoulder, and chest cage.
Careful inspection of the patient can provide important clues to the extent of the injury.
Considerable bruising and swelling may indicate deep trauma with possible vascular injury, which can be confirmed by evaluating the radial, ulnar, and brachial pulses.
A detailed neurologic examination is warranted to ascertain the location of the lesion.
Findings on initial examination are supplemented with advanced imaging and electrophysiological evaluation to determine the prognosis of neurologic recovery further.
additional_information.pre_op_preparation: Give thorough wound wash after administering adequate analgesia; Provide prophylactic antibiotics
additional_information.post_op_care: Advice strict hand elevation; The patient should be closely followed with serial examinations, imaging studies, and electrophysiological testing.
contraindications: Patient is not medically or psychologically cleared for surgery
Local wounds or infections obstruct the surgical approach
